title: salix myricoides var. albovestita
intro: Salix myricoides var. albovestita, also known as the white-barked willow, is a variety of shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive white or gray bark and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which make it useful for relieving pain and reducing swelling. The bark and leaves of Salix myricoides var. albovestita contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides its medicinal benefits. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and skin irritations.
